0.q: GST plays a pivotal role in detoxiﬁcation by conjugating glutathione (GSH) to cytotoxic compounds, thereby neutralizing peroxides; HaGST3.2 silencing in sunﬂower decreases salt tolerance.

1.q: Moreover, HaMYB120 and HaMYB181 synergistically strengthen HaMYB22‐mediated HaGST3.2 activation.

2.q: Moreover, HaMYB120 and HaMYB181 synergistically strengthen HaMYB22‐mediated HaGST3.2 activation.

3.q: Glutathione S-transferase HaGST3.2 was identified as a direct target of HaMYB22, and superior haplotype HaMYB22hap1 can strongly increase HaGST3.2 transcripts.

4.q: Further analysis showed that the Na+ content of transgenic Arabidopsis plants decreased under salt stress, which indicates that PM H+ ATPase participates in the physiological process of Na+ efflux, resulting in salt resistance of the plants.

5.q: HaWSD9 complemented the phenotype by producing wax ester but not TAG in vivo, indicating that it functions as a wax ester synthase.

6.q: Protein interaction assays revealed that HaMYB22 interacts with HaMYB120 and HaMYB181.

11.q: We expressed two sunflower oleate Δ12 desaturases encoded by FAD2-1 and FAD2-3 in yeast cells... Production of the heterologous desaturases increased the content of dienoic fatty acids.
